metadata.notes: Voice rewrite to Wingspan structured format — Opus, March 2026. Sources: official Stonemaier rulebook (Essential Edition), BGG FAQ, r/boardgames strategy threads.

key_rulings.text: Your personal score is the LOWER of your two city scores. The player with the highest personal score wins.
Both partners must agree on where to place tiles in their shared city. If partners cannot agree, the player whose turn it is to break ties decides.
Shops score based on straight lines (same row or column): 1 shop = 2 pts, 2 = 5 pts, 3 = 10 pts, 4 = 15 pts, 5 = 20 pts.
Factories score based on ranking across ALL cities: most factories = 4 pts each, second-most = 3 pts each, third-most = 2 pts each, all others = 1 pt each.
Taverns score by number of different subtypes: 1 type = 1 pt, 2 types = 4 pts, 3 types = 9 pts, 4 types (complete set) = 17 pts.
Each house scores points equal to the number of OTHER unique tile types present in the city, not counting houses themselves.
Duplex tiles must be placed as a 1x2 domino (horizontal or vertical). Both halves go into the same city and cannot be split.
In Round 1, pass remaining tiles to the LEFT after each selection.
In Round 3, pass remaining tiles to the RIGHT after each selection.
If tied on personal score, the player whose higher-scoring city has more points wins.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Tiles
- **108 single building tiles** — Square tiles in 6 types (18 of each), each with its own color and scoring rule:
  - **Shops** (blue) — Score by **straight lines** in the same row or column.
  - **Factories** (gray) — Score by **competitive ranking** across all cities in the game.
  - **Parks** (green) — Score by **connected groups** (tiles sharing a side).
  - **Taverns** (red) — Come in 4 subtypes (music, drink, food, bed). Score by **variety** of different subtypes.
  - **Offices** (purple) — Score by **cumulative count** in the city, with a bonus for being adjacent to taverns.
  - **Houses** (brown/yellow) — Score by **diversity** (each house is worth points equal to the number of other unique tile types in the city).
- **24 duplex tiles** — Larger tiles showing 2 building types side by side. Each takes up 2 squares in your city grid. Used only in Round 2.

#### Markers & Reference
- **7 city tokens** — Placed between neighboring players to mark each shared city.
- **1 scoreboard** — For tracking final city scores (0-80 range).
- **7 scoring markers** — One per player, placed on the scoreboard at the end.
- **7 player reference cards** — Double-sided cards showing all 6 tile scoring rules. Keep one in front of you.
- **1 seating randomizer card**

#### Automa (Solo/2-Player)
- **Automa tiles and reference card** — Solo and 2-player games only. Simulates extra players so drafting and dual-city mechanics work properly.

#### Other
- **1 rulebook** — Covers standard rules, solo rules, and Capitals expansion content (Essential Edition).

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
- **Semi-cooperative tile-drafting** — You build **two cities at once**, one shared with your left neighbor, one with your right neighbor.
- **City grid** — Each city is a **4x4 grid** of building tiles (16 squares total).
- **3 rounds** of play to fill every city.
- **Personal score** — The **lower** of your two city scores. You cannot pour everything into one city and ignore the other.

#### Setting Up
1. **Sit everyone in a circle** — Use the seating randomizer card to determine order.
2. **Place a city token** between each pair of neighbors to mark each shared city.
3. **Separate the tiles** — 108 single tiles in one face-down supply, 24 duplex tiles in another.
4. **Hand each player** a scoring marker and a reference card.
5. **Place the scoreboard** where everyone can reach it.

#### City Grid Layout
- Each city is a **4x4 grid** (4 rows x 4 columns, 16 total squares).
- **Single tiles** fill 1 square. **Duplex tiles** fill 2 adjacent squares (placed as a 1x2 domino, horizontal or vertical).
- Once placed, tiles **cannot be moved**.
- By game end, all 16 squares will be filled.

#### Checkpoint
- Each player has: a reference card and a scoring marker.
- Between every pair of neighbors: a city token.
- On the table: single tiles in one face-down supply, duplex tiles in another, scoreboard visible to all.

subtopics.title: Round-by-Round Flow
subtopics.content:
#### Round 1 — Single Tiles (Draft Left)
1. Each player draws **7 single tiles** from the supply. Keep hidden.
2. **Pick 2 tiles** simultaneously.
3. **Reveal** chosen tiles face-up. Place **1 tile in your left city**, **1 tile in your right city**. Discuss placement with each partner — you must **agree**.
4. **Pass remaining tiles** (5) to the player on your **left**.
5. Repeat: pick 2, reveal, place, pass left.
6. On the final pick (3 tiles remain): choose 2, **discard the last 1** face-down.

- After Round 1, each city has **5 single tiles** placed.

#### Round 2 — Duplex Tiles
1. Each player draws **3 duplex tiles** from the duplex supply.
2. **Keep 2**, **discard 1** face-down.
3. **Reveal** chosen duplex tiles. Place **1 duplex in your left city**, **1 duplex in your right city**.
- Each duplex occupies 2 squares, placed as a horizontal or vertical domino. Cannot split halves between cities.

- After Round 2, each city has 5 singles + 2 duplexes (4 squares) = **9 squares filled**.

#### Round 3 — Single Tiles (Draft Right)
1. Each player draws **7 single tiles** from the supply.
2. Same process as Round 1, but pass remaining tiles to the **right**.
3. On final pick, discard 1 tile face-down.

- After Round 3, every city is fully built — all **16 squares** filled.

#### Partnership Communication
- **Talk openly** with each partner about what the city needs.
- Discuss scoring opportunities, missing types, and tile placement.
- Good communication is a core part of the game.
- **Disagreement** — If partners cannot agree on placement, the player whose turn it is to break ties decides (alternates each round).

subtopics.title: Tile Scoring Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Shops (Blue) — Straight Line Scoring
- Score based on **straight lines** in the same row or column (not diagonal).
- 1 shop: **2 points**
- 2 shops in a line: **5 points**
- 3 shops in a line: **10 points**
- 4 shops in a line: **15 points**
- 5 shops in a line: **20 points** (possible with landscape mats)
- L, T, or + shapes: each shop counts toward only **one** line (pick the highest-scoring grouping).
- Multiple separate lines score independently.
- **Common mistake** — Scattering shops across different rows/columns. Three isolated shops = 6 points; three in a line = 10.

#### Factories (Gray) — Competitive Ranking
- Score by **competition across all cities** at the table:
  - **Most factories** (or tied for most): **4 points per factory**
  - **Second-most** (or tied): **3 points per factory**
  - **All others**: **2 points per factory**
- All cities tied = everyone scores 4 per factory.
- 0 factories = 0 points.
- **Key insight** — Placement within the grid does not matter. Only your **total count** relative to other cities matters.

#### Parks (Green) — Connected Group Scoring
- Score by **connected groups** (orthogonally adjacent, not diagonal). Any shape works (L, blob, cluster).
- 1 park: **2 points**
- 2 connected: **8 points**
- 3 connected: **12 points**
- 4 connected: **13 points**
- 5+ parks: **+1 point for each beyond 3**
- Multiple separate groups score independently.
- **Goal** — Connect all parks into one group if possible. Splitting costs points.

#### Taverns (Red) — Variety of Subtypes
- 4 subtypes: music, drink, food, bed (each has a different icon).
- Do NOT need to be adjacent.
- 1 subtype: **1 point**
- 2 different: **4 points**
- 3 different: **9 points**
- All 4 (complete set): **17 points**
- Extra taverns start a new set (e.g., 2 music + 1 food + 1 drink + 1 bed = 17 + 1 = **18 points**).
- **Common mistake** — Grabbing multiple taverns of the same subtype. Three music taverns still score only 1 point. You need **different** subtypes.

#### Offices (Purple) — Cumulative Count + Tavern Bonus
- Score by **total count** in the city (no adjacency required):
  - 1 office: **1 point** | 2: **3** | 3: **6** | 4: **10** | 5: **15** | 6: **21**
- **Tavern adjacency bonus** — Each office adjacent to at least **1 tavern** scores **+1 bonus point**.
- An office earns this bonus only once (even touching multiple taverns).
- A single tavern can grant the bonus to several adjacent offices.

#### Houses (Brown/Yellow) — Diversity Multiplier
- Each house scores points equal to the number of **other unique tile types** in the city (not counting houses).
- All 5 other types present = each house worth **5 points**.
- Only 3 other types = each house worth **3 points**.
- **Factory penalty** — A house **adjacent to a factory** drops to only **1 point total** regardless of diversity. Keep houses and factories separated.
- Even a single tile of a missing type adds 1 point to **every** house in the city.

subtopics.title: Scoring & Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### When the Game Ends
- The game lasts exactly **3 rounds** — no variable end trigger.
- After Round 3, every city grid is fully built. Move straight to scoring.

#### Scoring Each City
Score each city one at a time, going through all 6 tile types:
1. **Shops** — Find every straight line (same row or column). Apply line-length scoring.
2. **Factories** — Compare factory counts across **all** cities. Apply rank-based per-factory scoring.
3. **Parks** — Identify each connected group. Apply group-size scoring.
4. **Taverns** — Count different subtypes. Apply variety scoring (score extra sets separately).
5. **Offices** — Count total offices for cumulative scoring, then check each for tavern adjacency bonus (+1 each).
6. **Houses** — Count other unique tile types in the city. Multiply by number of houses (check for factory adjacency — those houses drop to 1 point).

- Add all 6 categories for the city's total score.

#### Your Personal Score
- **Personal score = the LOWER of your two city scores.**
- This is the central tension: you **must** balance effort between both cities.
- A 40-40 split (personal score: 40) beats a 55-25 split (personal score: 25).

#### Winning
- **Highest personal score** wins.

#### Tiebreakers
1. Tied player whose **higher-scoring city** has more points wins (rewards balance).
2. Still tied? **More combined total points** across both cities.
3. Still tied? **Shared victory**.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### The Golden Rule: Balance Your Cities
- **Your score = the LOWER city** — A 40-40 split (score: 40) beats a 55-25 split (score: 25).
- Every pick, ask: **which city needs more help right now?**
- When choosing 2 tiles, think about what **each** city needs. Picking 2 for one city neglects the other.

#### Tile Priority Guide
- **Houses (safest bet)** — With diversity (1 of every other type), each house scores 5 points. Three houses in a diverse city = 15 easy points. Rarely a bad pick.
- **Taverns (need commitment)** — 1 tavern = 1 point. 2 different = 4. Complete set of 4 subtypes = **17 points**. Either commit to variety or skip entirely.
- **Shops (want straight lines)** — Place in the same row or column and extend. 3 in a line = 10 points; 3 scattered = only 6. Decide direction early.
- **Parks (want connected clusters)** — Any shape works as long as tiles share a side. 3 connected = 12 points; 3 isolated = only 6.
- **Factories (a gamble)** — Most factories across all cities = 4 pts each (potentially huge). Getting out-counted drops you to 2-3 pts each. Only invest heavily if you can win or tie for top rank.
- **Offices + Taverns (synergy)** — Offices scale well (up to 21 for 6), and each office adjacent to a tavern earns +1 bonus. Place near each other.

#### Drafting Tips
- **Watch what you pass** — Tiles you do not pick go to your neighbor, who shares a city with you. Passing strong tiles to your left neighbor can directly help your shared left city in Round 1.
- **Hate-draft sparingly** — Denying distant opponents rarely matters as much as building your own cities well.
- **Establish a plan in Round 1** — First 2-3 placements set each city's direction. Decide early: heavy taverns? Heavy houses? Balanced mix?

#### Common Mistakes
- **Pouring best tiles into one city** — Your neglected city drags your personal score down.
- **Scattering shops and parks** — Isolated tiles score terribly. Shops need lines; parks need groups.
- **Ignoring house diversity** — 4 houses with 2 other types = 8 points. Same 4 houses with 5 other types = 20 points. Even one tile of a missing type adds 4 points.
- **Forgetting tavern subtypes** — 3 taverns of the **same** subtype score only 1 point. You need **different** subtypes.
- **Houses next to factories** — A house adjacent to a factory scores only 1 point instead of potentially 5. Keep them apart.
- **Not communicating** — Talk about what the city needs, what is coming in the draft, and where tiles should go. Silence leads to bad cities.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Between Two Cities

**Getting Oriented**: You sit in a circle. You share a city with your left neighbor and a different city with your right neighbor. Your personal score is the **lower** of the two. Grab your reference card showing all 6 tile scoring rules.

**Round 1, First Pick**
*Step 1*: Draw 7 single tiles. Fan them out and identify the types (colors).
*Step 2*: Pick 2 tiles — one for your left city, one for your right.
*Step 3*: Everyone reveals simultaneously. Talk to each partner about placement in the 4x4 grid.

**A good first-pick approach**: Place a house in each city if available. Houses are versatile and score well as long as you diversify later. If you spot 2 taverns of different subtypes, grab both — that is 4 points of variety scoring vs. 1 point for a single tavern.

**Round 1 Continues**
- Pass remaining 5 tiles to the left.
- Pick 2 from the new hand, reveal, place one in each city, pass left again.
- Do this 3 times total. On the last pick (3 tiles): choose 2, discard 1 face-down.
- Each city now has **5 single tiles**.

**Round 2 — Duplex Tiles**
- Draw 3 duplex tiles. Each shows 2 building types and covers 2 squares.
- Keep 2, discard 1. Place 1 duplex in each city.
- A well-placed duplex can simultaneously extend a shop line AND add a missing type for houses.

**Round 3 — Final Push**
- Draw 7 new single tiles. Same process as Round 1, but pass to the **right**.
- Last chance to fill scoring gaps:
  - Need that 4th tavern subtype for a 17-point set?
  - Need a park to bridge two disconnected groups?
  - Prioritize whatever closes the biggest gap in your **weaker** city.

**Scoring**
- Score each city type by type using your reference card.
- Add all 6 categories for each city's total.
- Your personal score = the **lower** city score.
- For a first game: **40-50 points** is solid. **55+** is excellent.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### The Partner Paradox
- **Your score = your LOWER city** — A 60-point masterpiece paired with a 30-point disaster gives you a personal score of 30.
- Always invest in whichever city is currently weaker, even if the stronger city has a tempting opportunity.

#### Tile Valuation by Game State
- **Factories** — Score by competitive ranking. 3 factories leading the table = 12 points (4 each). Same 3 in third place = 6 points (2 each). Watch the table: if nobody else collects factories, even 2-3 can win top rank cheaply.
- **Shops** — Want **straight lines** in the same row or column. Line of 3 = 10 points; line of 4 = 15. Plan direction in Round 1 and protect that row/column.
- **Taverns** — Pure set collection: 1/4/9/17 points for 1/2/3/4 unique subtypes. The jump from 3 types (9 pts) to a complete set (17 pts) is massive. At 3 subtypes by Round 3, that missing 4th is worth hunting hard for.
- **Houses** — Score based on **other** building types present. Strongest in diverse cities, weakest in specialized ones. A single tile of a missing type adds 1 point to **every** house.

#### Draft Reading
- Pay attention to what neighbors are drafting.
- **Left neighbor collecting factories?** Feed factories into your shared city — you both benefit from a high count.
- **Right neighbor ignoring taverns?** Do not pour tavern tiles into a city where your partner will not help finish the set.

#### Duplex Strategy
- Duplex tiles cover 2 squares and cannot be repositioned.
- In Round 2, your grid is only partially filled (5 of 16 squares) — use the flexibility.
- A well-placed duplex can simultaneously extend a shop line, connect park groups, or add a missing type for houses.
- **Best duplexes** combine two types your city needs (like Park/Tavern when missing both).

#### Round 3 Awareness
- Each city has 9 of 16 squares filled. You can see what every city is building.
- **Factory count check** — Count factories in all visible cities. If close to the lead, one more could jump from 2 pts/factory to 4 pts/factory (swing of 6-8 points).
- **Fill gaps in your weaker city first** — Your stronger city can absorb a mediocre tile; your weaker city cannot.
- **Draft direction** — Round 3 passes right (opposite of Round 1). Tiles you pass go to the player sharing your **right** city.

#### The Kingmaker Trap
- In final picks, consider the scoreboard.
- If you cannot win, avoid placements that hand victory to one specific opponent over another.
- Balanced, self-interested play earns trust at the table — in a partnership game, that matters for future rounds and games.
